data.narrative:
Neuralink, a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Elon Musk and eight co-founders, has raised $1.3B+ and carries a current valuation of $9.7B. The peer universe for this analysis comprises four same-sector, same-stage private companies: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), and Groq ($7.0B), with one public comparable, Palantir Technologies.

Peer-median valuation analysis represents the applicable triangulation method. The four-company peer cohort yields a median valuation of $106.2B, with a range spanning $7.0B at the low end (Groq) to $300.0B at the high end (OpenAI). This substantial dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business models, technology maturity, and market positioning within the AI & Software sector, even among late-stage private firms.

The triangulated valuation range derived from peer-median methodology is $90.2B (low), $106.2B (median), and $122.1B (high). Neuralink's current $9.7B valuation sits substantially below this implied range. Key uncertainties include the applicability of generalist AI software comparables to Neuralink's specialized neurotechnology focus, the capital intensity and regulatory pathway unique to medical device deployment, and the limited salability of late-stage AI valuations given recent market repricing. A single-method analysis warrants caution; additional revenue-based or comparable-transaction methods would strengthen conviction.
